command.short: Exercise ax-go primitives from a real Cobra command
command.example:
ax-integration --format=json --name Ada
  OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT=http://localhost:4318 ax-integration --format=json
  AX_OTEL_DEBUG=1 ax-integration --format=json
  ax-integration --config=config.hujson
  ax-integration stream --count=3
  ax-integration patch-config --config=config.hujson --patch='[{"op":"replace","path":"/count","value":5}]'
  ax-integration fail
  ax-integration fetch
  ax-integration authz
  ax-integration crash
  ax-integration __schema

commands.short: Generate the autocompletion script for the specified shell
commands.long:
Generate the autocompletion script for ax-integration for the specified shell.
See each sub-command's help for details on how to use the generated script.

flags.name: dry-run
flags.usage: emit the envelope without side effects

flags.name: format
flags.usage: output format: json or human

flags.name: idempotency-key
flags.usage: opaque key used to prevent duplicate-create retries

commands.short: Generate the autocompletion script for bash
commands.long:
Generate the autocompletion script for the bash shell.

This script depends on the 'bash-completion' package.
If it is not installed already, you can install it via your OS's package manager.

To load completions in your current shell session:

	source <(ax-integration completion bash)

To load completions for every new session, execute once:

#### Linux:

	ax-integration completion bash > /etc/bash_completion.d/ax-integration

#### macOS:

	ax-integration completion bash > $(brew --prefix)/etc/bash_completion.d/ax-integration

You will need to start a new shell for this setup to take effect.

flags.name: no-descriptions
flags.usage: disable completion descriptions

flags.name: dry-run
flags.usage: emit the envelope without side effects

flags.name: format
flags.usage: output format: json or human

flags.name: idempotency-key
flags.usage: opaque key used to prevent duplicate-create retries

commands.short: Generate the autocompletion script for fish
commands.long:
Generate the autocompletion script for the fish shell.

To load completions in your current shell session:

	ax-integration completion fish | source

To load completions for every new session, execute once:

	ax-integration completion fish > ~/.config/fish/completions/ax-integration.fish

You will need to start a new shell for this setup to take effect.

flags.usage: disable completion descriptions

commands.short: Generate the autocompletion script for powershell
commands.long:
Generate the autocompletion script for powershell.

To load completions in your current shell session:

	ax-integration completion powershell | Out-String | Invoke-Expression

To load completions for every new session, add the output of the above command
to your powershell profile.

flags.name: no-descriptions
flags.usage: disable completion descriptions

flags.name: dry-run
flags.usage: emit the envelope without side effects

flags.name: format
flags.usage: output format: json or human

flags.name: idempotency-key
flags.usage: opaque key used to prevent duplicate-create retries

commands.short: Generate the autocompletion script for zsh
commands.long:
Generate the autocompletion script for the zsh shell.

If shell completion is not already enabled in your environment you will need
to enable it.  You can execute the following once:

	echo "autoload -U compinit; compinit" >> ~/.zshrc

To load completions in your current shell session:

	source <(ax-integration completion zsh)

To load completions for every new session, execute once:

#### Linux:

	ax-integration completion zsh > "${fpath[1]}/_ax-integration"

#### macOS:

	ax-integration completion zsh > $(brew --prefix)/share/zsh/site-functions/_ax-integration

You will need to start a new shell for this setup to take effect.

commands.short: Run this CLI as a live MCP server
commands.long: Expose this CLI's command tree as a live Model Context Protocol (MCP) server. Every non-hidden command (minus the reserved __schema, mcp-server, and completion commands) becomes a tool; tools/call runs the command in machine mode and returns its payload. Serves over stdio by default, or streamable HTTP (loopback unless --allow-non-loopback is set).
commands.example:
mycli mcp-server
  mycli mcp-server --transport=http --addr=127.0.0.1:8080
  mycli mcp-server --transport=http --addr=0.0.0.0:8080 --allow-non-loopback

flags.name: addr
flags.usage: HTTP bind address; loopback by default (used with --transport=http)

flags.name: allow-non-loopback
flags.usage: allow the HTTP transport to bind a non-loopback address (fail-closed without it)

flags.name: transport
flags.usage: MCP transport: stdio or http

flags.name: dry-run
flags.usage: emit the envelope without side effects

flags.name: format
flags.usage: output format: json or human

flags.name: idempotency-key
flags.usage: opaque key used to prevent duplicate-create retries
